story.asA: developer building Chiron's workflow orchestration system
story.iWant: a generic workflow execution engine that loads workflows from the database, executes steps sequentially, manages state, and resolves variables
story.soThat: all future BMAD workflows can be executed without custom code, enabling runtime workflow modification and consistent execution behavior across all methodology phases
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Workflow Loader Service (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4) - [ ] **Subtask 1.1:** Create `packages/api/src/services/workflow-engine/workflow-loader.ts` - Implement `loadWorkflow(workflowId: string)` function, query workflows + workflow_steps, sort by stepNumber ascending, return structured object - [ ] **Subtask 1.2:** Implement workflow structure validation - Allow gaps in step numbers (1, 3, 5...) with warning - Verify nextStepNumber references point to valid steps - Detect cycles: BLOCK if no branch steps, WARN if cycle has branch (state machine pattern OK) - Hybrid cycle detection: Static warning + runtime 100-step execution limit - Unknown step types: Load all steps, warn during load, auto-advance using nextStepNumber at runtime - [ ] **Subtask 1.3:** Write unit tests for workflow loader (gap detection, cycle detection, unknown types)
### Task 2: Step Executor Framework (AC: #5, #6, #7, #8, #9) - [ ] **Subtask 2.1:** Define step handler interface in `packages/api/src/services/workflow-engine/step-handler.ts` - [ ] **Subtask 2.2:** Create STEP_HANDLERS type map in `packages/api/src/services/workflow-engine/step-types.ts` - Export const STEP_HANDLERS object with all handler classes - Derive StepType union from STEP_HANDLERS keys for type safety - Use in DB schema validation and step registry - [ ] **Subtask 2.3:** Implement step type registry in `packages/api/src/services/workflow-engine/step-registry.ts` - Initialize registry from STEP_HANDLERS map at startup - Centralized handler registration for type safety - [ ] **Subtask 2.4:** Implement step execution loop in `packages/api/src/services/workflow-engine/executor.ts` - Track step execution count, enforce 100-step limit - Handle unknown step types: warn, auto-advance using nextStepNumber - Error handling: Check errorHandling.skipOnFailure config, halt or skip accordingly - Add TODO comment for optimistic locking (Epic 6) - [ ] **Subtask 2.5:** Write unit tests for step executor (unknown types, error handling, execution limit)
### Task 3: Variable Resolver (AC: #10-#15) - [ ] **Subtask 3.1:** Install deepmerge: `bun add deepmerge` and `bun add -D @types/deepmerge` - [ ] **Subtask 3.2:** Implement variable resolver with Handlebars in `packages/api/src/services/workflow-engine/variable-resolver.ts` - Use Handlebars.compile for {{variable}} syntax - Support nested access: {{user.name}}, {{array[0]}}, {{step_4.recommended_track}} - Support Handlebars helpers: #each, #if, #with (built-in, no custom helpers in Story 1.4) - [ ] **Subtask 3.3:** Define execution context type in `packages/api/src/services/workflow-engine/execution-context.ts` - Level 1: System variables (current_user_id, execution_id, date, timestamp) - Level 2: Execution variables (user inputs + LLM outputs + system-executed values) - Level 3: Step outputs (from executedSteps JSONB) - Level 4: Default values (from step config) - Minimal context: no db/llmClient (handlers import directly from @chiron/db) - [ ] **Subtask 3.4:** Implement precedence merging logic (Level 1 > Level 2 > Level 3 > Level 4) - Merge using object spread for precedence - No custom template caching in Story 1.4 (keep simple) - [ ] **Subtask 3.5:** Write unit tests for variable resolver (nested variables, precedence levels, missing variables)
### Task 4: State Management (AC: #16-#22) - [ ] **Subtask 4.1:** Implement workflow execution state creation in `packages/api/src/services/workflow-engine/state-manager.ts` - Create workflow_executions record on workflow start (status: "running") - Initialize empty executedSteps JSONB object - Add TODO comment: "Future enhancement - Save partial input for better UX (deferred to chat steps Story 1.6+)" - [ ] **Subtask 4.2:** Implement step completion tracking - Update executedSteps JSONB - executedSteps key: step number (execution order) e.g., "1", "2", "3" - executedSteps value: { stepId (UUID reference), status, startedAt, completedAt, output } - Deep merge variables using deepmerge library: deepmerge(execution.variables, result.output) - Add TODO comment: "Add optimistic locking for concurrent protection (Epic 6 multi-agent)" - [ ] **Subtask 4.3:** Implement pause/resume functionality - Pause: Set status to "paused", save currentStep - Resume: Validate status, set to "running", continue from currentStep with userInput - [ ] **Subtask 4.4:** Implement workflow completion/failure handlers - Complete: status = "completed", set completedAt timestamp - Error: status = "error", populate error text field - skipOnFailure logic: Mark step as failed in executedSteps, continue to nextStepNumber - [ ] **Subtask 4.5:** Write unit tests for state manager (deep merge, pause/resume, error handling)
### Task 5: Event System (AC: #23-#25) - [ ] **Subtask 5.1:** Implement event bus in `packages/api/src/services/workflow-engine/event-bus.ts` - Use Node EventEmitter for internal event bus - Define WorkflowEvent type union with 7 core events - Minimal event payloads: executionId, stepNumber, timestamp (no full output data) - [ ] **Subtask 5.2:** Emit events in executor - workflow_started, step_started, step_completed, workflow_paused, workflow_resumed, workflow_completed, workflow_error - No event persistence (executedSteps JSONB serves as audit trail) - [ ] **Subtask 5.3:** Implement tRPC SSE subscription endpoint in `workflowRouter` - Use tRPC .subscription() with observable - Filter events by executionId - tRPC handles SSE transport automatically (no WebSocket config needed in Story 1.4) - [ ] **Subtask 5.4:** Frontend tRPC subscription hook - Use trpc.workflows.onWorkflowEvent.useSubscription - On event, invalidate getExecution query to fetch full step data - [ ] **Subtask 5.5:** Write unit tests for event system (emit events, subscription filtering)
### Task 6: UI Components - Wizard Stepper Pattern (AC: #26-#28) - [ ] **Subtask 6.1:** Install framer-motion: `bun add framer-motion` - [ ] **Subtask 6.2:** Create shared stepper types in `apps/web/src/components/workflows/types.ts` - [ ] **Subtask 6.3:** Create IconWrapper component in `apps/web/src/components/ui/icon-wrapper.tsx` - [ ] **Subtask 6.4:** Create WorkflowStepperWizard component in `apps/web/src/components/workflows/steppers/wizard/workflow-stepper-wizard.tsx` - [ ] **Subtask 6.5:** Create WizardStepContainer component in `apps/web/src/components/workflows/steppers/wizard/wizard-step-container.tsx` - [ ] **Subtask 6.6:** Create example step content components - [ ] **Subtask 6.7:** Implement auto-advance logic in executor - [ ] **Subtask 6.8:** Write component tests (Vitest + React Testing Library)
### Task 7: Integration & Testing (AC: #29-#35) - [ ] **Subtask 7.1:** End-to-end integration test for empty workflow - [ ] **Subtask 7.2:** Error handling integration test - [ ] **Subtask 7.3:** Performance validation tests - [ ] **Subtask 7.4:** Manual testing checklist

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Workflow Loader:** - [ ] **AC1:** Workflow loader service reads workflow + steps from database by workflow ID - [ ] **AC2:** Steps loaded in correct order (sorted by stepNumber ascending) - [ ] **AC3:** Workflow structure validated (no missing steps, valid nextStepNumber references, no cycles) - [ ] **AC4:** Validation errors thrown with clear messages
**Step Executor Framework:** - [ ] **AC5:** Generic step handler interface defined: executeStep(step, context) → result - [ ] **AC6:** Step type registry maps step types to handler implementations - [ ] **AC7:** Step execution loop executes steps sequentially, respecting nextStepNumber - [ ] **AC8:** Step transitions support auto-advance and wait-for-user-input modes - [ ] **AC9:** Unrecognized step type throws clear error
**Variable Resolver:** - [ ] **AC10:** Variable resolver uses Handlebars templating for {{variable}} syntax - [ ] **AC11:** 4-level precedence implemented: System → Execution → Step outputs → Defaults - [ ] **AC12:** First match wins (Level 1 before Level 2, etc.) - [ ] **AC13:** Nested variables supported: {{user.name}}, {{array[0]}}, {{step_4.recommended_track}} - [ ] **AC14:** Missing required variable throws error - [ ] **AC15:** Handlebars templates compiled and cached
**State Management:** - [ ] **AC16:** workflow_executions record created on workflow start (status: "running") - [ ] **AC17:** currentStep field updated after each step completion - [ ] **AC18:** executedSteps JSONB field tracks step-by-step history - [ ] **AC19:** Can pause workflow mid-execution (status: "paused") - [ ] **AC20:** Can resume paused workflow from last completed step - [ ] **AC21:** Workflow completion sets status to "completed" and completedAt timestamp - [ ] **AC22:** Workflow errors set status to "error" and populate error text field
**Event System:** - [ ] **AC23:** Events emitted at lifecycle transitions (workflow_started, step_started, step_completed, workflow_completed, workflow_error) - [ ] **AC24:** Event system uses Node EventEmitter for internal event bus - [ ] **AC25:** tRPC SSE subscription endpoint for real-time events
**UI Components:** - [ ] **AC26:** WorkflowStepper component displays step X of N, progress bar, current step title/goal - [ ] **AC27:** WorkflowStepContainer component: wrapper with Next/Back buttons, loading state, error display - [ ] **AC28:** Auto-advance for backend-only steps
**Testing:** - [ ] **AC29:** Unit tests for workflow engine services (load, execute, resolve variables, state management) - [ ] **AC30:** Integration test: Start → pause → resume - [ ] **AC31:** Error handling test: Invalid step type triggers clear error - [ ] **AC32:** Variable resolution test: Nested variables, missing variables
**Performance:** - [ ] **AC33:** Step transitions execute within 200ms - [ ] **AC34:** Variable resolution completes within 50ms for typical templates - [ ] **AC35:** Database queries for workflow loading complete within 100ms

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: FR002: System shall execute workflows following BMAD's workflow.xml engine rules (steps, actions, templates, elicitation). FR004: System shall resolve variables using 4-level precedence (config_source, system-generated, user input, defaults). FR005: System shall maintain workflow state and enable resume/restart of interrupted workflows.

artifact.title: Epic Breakdown
artifact.snippet: Story 1.4: Workflow Execution Engine Core. Build the generic workflow execution engine foundation that will power all workflows in Chiron. This is the core service that loads workflows from the database, executes steps sequentially, manages state, and resolves variables.

artifact.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: Workflow Engine Service: Load workflows from DB, execute steps sequentially, manage state, resolve variables. Step Handler Registry: Register and dispatch step type handlers. Variable Resolver: Resolve {{variable}} references using Handlebars with 4-level precedence. Event Bus: Emit workflow events for real-time UI updates.

artifact.title: Database Schema Architecture
artifact.snippet: workflow_executions table: Runtime workflow execution state with executedSteps JSONB tracking step-by-step history. Dual Progress Tracking: executedSteps (step-level) + executedVsPath (workflow-level) provide complete visibility.

artifact.title: BMAD Workflow Engine Rules
artifact.snippet: Execute ALL steps in instructions IN EXACT ORDER. Steps execute in exact numerical order (1, 2, 3...). Optional steps: Ask user unless #yolo mode active. Template-output tags: Save content → Show user → Get approval before continuing.

artifact.reason: Core workflow loader service - reads workflows and steps from database, validates structure
Main execution loop - orchestrates step-by-step workflow execution with state management
Generic step handler interface that all step type handlers must implement
Registry mapping step types to handler implementations for extensibility
Handlebars-based variable resolution with 4-level precedence system
Manages workflow execution state persistence and resume capability
Event system using Node EventEmitter for workflow lifecycle events
Existing tRPC router index - will add workflows router here
Database schema for workflows - already created in Story 1.1
Shared TypeScript types for all stepper variants
Reusable component for corner bracket decoration on icons
Horizontal progress bar stepper for quick linear workflows
Animated step wrapper with navigation, loading, and error states

node.package: Template engine for variable resolution with {{variable}} syntax
Deep merge utility for execution variables (lightweight, battle-tested)

story-context.constraints: - Workflow engine MUST be generic and reusable for all future workflows (not workflow-init-specific) - Step handlers registered via hybrid approach: centralized STEP_HANDLERS type map for type safety - JSONB executedSteps field tracks complete execution history with stepId reference for audit trail - Variable resolution: Handlebars with 4-level precedence (System > Execution > Step Outputs > Defaults) - Level 2 (Execution Variables) includes both user inputs AND LLM-generated values (decisions, artifacts) - Deep merge execution variables using deepmerge library (preserves nested objects) - Workflow loader validation: Allow step gaps (warn), block pure cycles, warn on cycles with branches - Unknown step types: Load all steps, auto-advance using nextStepNumber at runtime with warning - Error handling: Config-driven skipOnFailure boolean (no retry logic in Story 1.4) - State persistence after each step completion ensures recovery from crashes - TODO: Add optimistic locking for concurrent execution protection (Epic 6 multi-agent) - No partial input saving in Story 1.4 (deferred to chat step implementation) - Event system: tRPC SSE subscriptions with minimal payloads (IDs only, frontend fetches details) - 7 core events: workflow_started, step_started, step_completed, workflow_paused, workflow_resumed, workflow_completed, workflow_error - No event persistence (executedSteps JSONB serves as audit trail) - Runtime limit: 100 step executions per workflow prevents infinite loops - Services (db, llmClient) imported directly by handlers, not passed in ExecutionContext - Use Bun test framework for all testing - Follow existing project patterns from Stories 1.1-1.3 (tRPC routers, service layer, React components)

interface.name: StepHandler
ExecutionContext
workflowRouter
WorkflowStepperWizard

tests.standards: All tests use Bun test framework (`bun test`). Unit tests for each service in isolation with mocked dependencies. Integration tests for full workflow execution with real database. Component tests for UI with mocked tRPC queries. Performance tests for variable resolution and step transitions. Story 1.4 focuses on empty workflow (0 steps) - step handlers will be tested in Stories 1.5-1.8.
tests.ideas: - Test AC29-1: Load workflow-init-new from database (metadata only, 0 steps for Story 1.4) → executeWorkflow should complete immediately for empty workflow - Test AC29-2: Execute empty workflow (0 steps) successfully → verify workflow_executions record created with status "completed" - Test AC29-3: Variable resolution with all 4 precedence levels → test that system variables override execution variables, etc. - Test AC29-4: Workflow state saves/loads from database → verify executedSteps JSONB persists correctly - Test AC30: Start workflow → pause → resume from correct step → verify currentStep and executedSteps restored correctly - Test AC31: Invalid step type triggers clear error → register only "ask-user" handler, try to execute "unknown-step", expect UnknownStepTypeError - Test AC32: Variable resolution edge cases → nested variables ({{user.name}}), array access ({{items[0]}}), missing variables (expect error) - Test AC33-35: Performance validation → measure step transition time, variable resolution time, database query time - Component Test: WorkflowStepperWizard displays correct progress (e.g., "Step 3 of 10") and visual progress bar - Component Test: WizardStepContainer shows Back/Continue buttons and loading state during step execution
